mnemonics.title: TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE COMPONENTS
mnemonics.content: HARDWARE SOFTWARE TOOLS - REMEMBER: H.O.S.T. FOR WAREHOUSE SUCCESS: Hardware, Operating System, Storage, Tools (ETL, Reporting, Metadata)

mnemonics.title: METADATA TYPES
mnemonics.content: TECHNICAL BUSINESS PROCESS - TBP: Technical (schemas, ETL specs), Business (definitions), Process (lineage, history)

mnemonics.title: ETL PROCESS STEPS
mnemonics.content: EXTRACT THEN TRANSFORM LOAD - E.T.L.: Extract (from sources), Transform (clean, convert), Load (into warehouse)

acronyms.usage: The process of moving data from source systems to the data warehouse
Architecture using multiple processors to handle large datasets in parallel
Alternative to ETL where transformation happens within the database after loading
Technique for tracking changes in dimension data over time in the warehouse
Intermediate storage area for operational data before warehouse loading

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a LAYERED CAKE: Bottom layer = Hardware/OS, Middle layer = Database Software, Top layer = Tools/Applications. Each layer supports the one above it.
Think of a FACTORY ASSEMBLY LINE: Raw materials (source data) → Receiving dock (Staging) → Processing station (Transform) → Warehouse (Storage) → Shipping (Delivery to users).
Imagine MULTIPLE WORKERS at different desks (nodes) sharing the same workload rather than one super worker - distributes effort for faster completion.
Think of METADATA AS A MAP: Just as a map tells you about roads, distances, and landmarks, metadata tells you about data sources, relationships, and meanings.

analogies.analogy: Like a KITCHEN APPLIANCE PACKAGE: Instead of buying separate oven, refrigerator, and microwave, you get a pre-matched set that works together optimally.
Like a LOADING DOCK at a warehouse: Goods arrive, are checked, sorted, and processed before going into main storage - provides a buffer and error-checking point.
Like a LIBRARY CATALOG: The actual books (data) are in the library, but the catalog (metadata) tells you where each book is, what it's about, and how to find it.
Like LEGO BLOCKS: Each component (storage, processing, tools) is a separate block that can be swapped, upgraded, or replaced without rebuilding the entire structure.
Like a RESTAURANT KITCHEN: Ingredients arrive raw (extract), get washed, cut, cooked (transform), then plated and served (load) to customers.
